title: Form 35.1 — Affidavit in Support of Claim for Custody or Access
description: Required in every Ontario family law case where a party claims decision-making responsibility (custody) or parenting time (access). Must be sworn and filed with your application or motion. Requires full disclosure of past involvement with child protection services, criminal history, and domestic violence history.

steps.title: About This Affidavit
steps.description: This affidavit is required by law whenever you ask for decision-making responsibility (custody) or parenting time (access) in Ontario.

fields.label: Important notice before you begin
fields.content:
**This form requires you to disclose sensitive personal information.** Ontario law requires anyone asking for custody or access to disclose:

- Any involvement with a Children's Aid Society (CAS) in Ontario or elsewhere
- Any criminal charges or convictions
- Any history of domestic violence or abuse

This information is kept in the court file and shared with the other party. **You must answer all questions honestly.** Providing false information in an affidavit is perjury, which is a criminal offence.

This affidavit must be sworn before a commissioner of oaths — do not sign it until you are in front of the commissioner.

fields.label: Courthouse
options: St. Catharines — Superior Court of Justice
Thunder Bay — Superior Court of Justice

fields.label: Court file number
Applicant's full legal name
Respondent's full legal name
Your full legal name (person swearing this affidavit)
fields.helpText: This is the person making the sworn statement — usually the applicant or respondent filing this form.

fields.label: You are

steps.title: The Children
steps.description: List each child for whom you are claiming decision-making responsibility or parenting time.

fields.label: Children involved in this claim
fields.helpText: List every child for whom you are asking for custody or access.

fields.label: Child's full legal name
fields.placeholder: e.g. Alex Jordan Smith

fields.label: Date of birth
Child's current address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 123 Main St, Toronto ON M5V 1A1
fields.helpText: Where the child lives right now.

fields.label: Child currently lives with
Name of the other person the child lives with
fields.placeholder: e.g. Maternal grandmother — Mary Johnson

fields.label: What are you asking for regarding this child?

options.label: Primary parenting time (child lives primarily with me)

steps.title: Your Living Situation
steps.description: Describe where you currently live and who lives with you.

fields.label: Your current address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 456 Oak Ave, Toronto ON M6G 2B3

fields.label: How long have you lived at this address?
fields.placeholder: e.g. 8 months / since March 2025

fields.label: Who else lives in your home?
fields.helpText: List everyone who lives with you other than the children named above. Include new partners, other children, parents, roommates, etc.

fields.label: Full name
fields.placeholder: e.g. Michael Brown

fields.label: Relationship to you
fields.placeholder: e.g. New partner, my mother, adult sibling, roommate

fields.label: Age
Do you plan to move in the next 12 months?
Describe your planned move
fields.placeholder: e.g. I plan to move to Ottawa in September 2025 for a new job. I intend to bring the children with me and enroll them in school there.

steps.title: Previous Court Proceedings
steps.description: Disclose any previous or ongoing court cases involving these children or yourself.

fields.label: Have there been any previous court proceedings about the custody, access, or child protection of the children named in this form?
fields.helpText: Include proceedings in Ontario or any other province, territory, or country.

fields.label: Describe the previous court proceedings
Court and location
fields.placeholder: e.g. Ontario Court of Justice, Toronto / Family Court, Brampton

fields.label: File number (if known)
Type of proceeding
fields.placeholder: e.g. Custody and access application / Child protection / Divorce

fields.label: Outcome or current status
fields.placeholder: e.g. Order dated June 1, 2023 gave the Applicant primary custody with Respondent having access every other weekend. Case is now closed. / Still ongoing.

fields.label: Are there any existing court orders or agreements about the children?
Describe the existing orders or agreements
fields.placeholder: e.g. There is a temporary consent order dated April 10, 2025 providing that the children live primarily with the Applicant. There is also a separation agreement dated January 2024 that addresses parenting but which I am now seeking to vary.

steps.title: Child Protection Involvement
steps.description: You must disclose any involvement with Children's Aid Societies (CAS) or equivalent child protection agencies — in Ontario or anywhere else.

fields.label: Important
fields.content: You must disclose **all** past or current involvement with Children's Aid Societies (CAS), child protection agencies, or equivalent government bodies — in Ontario or any other province, territory, or country. This includes investigations that did not result in a finding or that were closed. Failure to disclose is taken very seriously by courts.

fields.label: Have you or anyone in your household ever been involved with a Children's Aid Society or child protection agency?
Describe each involvement with a child protection agency
fields.helpText: Include all investigations, protection orders, voluntary services agreements, or court proceedings.

fields.label: Name of the CAS or child protection agency
fields.placeholder: e.g. Children's Aid Society of Toronto / Ministry for Children in British Columbia

fields.label: Approximate date or period of involvement
fields.placeholder: e.g. March to August 2021 / 2019 (date unknown)

fields.label: Reason for the involvement
fields.placeholder: e.g. A neighbour made an anonymous report of neglect. A worker investigated and found no concerns. The file was closed after two home visits.

fields.label: Outcome
fields.placeholder: e.g. File closed with no findings / Voluntary service agreement signed and completed / Child apprehended and returned following court order / Protection order made — specify terms

fields.label: Who was involved (you, your partner, your child, another household member)?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Myself and the child Alex / My current partner (before we met)

fields.label: Is there currently an open child protection case involving you or any child in your home?
Describe the current open child protection case
fields.placeholder: e.g. There is a current open file with the CAS of Peel regarding an allegation made by the other party in January 2025. The worker has conducted two visits and the investigation is ongoing. No protection concerns have been identified to date.

steps.title: Criminal History
steps.description: You must disclose any criminal charges or convictions. This includes offences related to violence, weapons, drugs, and any offences involving children.

fields.label: Important
fields.content: You must disclose **all** criminal charges and convictions — including those for which you received a pardon (record suspension), charges that were withdrawn or stayed, and charges that are currently before the courts. Disclose charges in Canada and any other country.

fields.label: Have you ever been charged with or convicted of a criminal offence in Canada or any other country?
List each criminal charge or conviction
Offence description
fields.placeholder: e.g. Assault / Impaired driving / Possession of a controlled substance

fields.label: Approximate date of the offence or charge
fields.placeholder: e.g. June 2018 / Approximately 2015

fields.label: Outcome

options.label: Convicted and received a pardon / record suspension

fields.label: Sentence or conditions (if convicted)
fields.placeholder: e.g. Fine of $500 / 6-month conditional sentence / 18 months probation

fields.label: Did this offence involve violence, weapons, or children?
Does anyone else in your household have a criminal record or pending charges?
Describe the household member's criminal history
fields.placeholder: e.g. My current partner was convicted of impaired driving in 2019 and received a fine. There have been no other charges.

steps.title: Domestic Violence & Abuse History
steps.description: You must disclose any history of domestic violence, abuse, or a restraining order — whether you were the victim, the alleged perpetrator, or both.

fields.label: About this section
fields.content:
Ontario courts take domestic violence very seriously in custody and access decisions. You must disclose:

- Any history of physical, sexual, emotional, psychological, or financial abuse between you and the other party
- Any police involvement or criminal charges related to domestic violence
- Any restraining orders or peace bonds
- Any history of stalking, harassment, or coercive control

This information helps the court assess the safety of the children and both parties. Disclosing abuse does not automatically change your right to parenting time — the court's focus is on the children's safety and best interests.

fields.label: Has there been any domestic violence or abuse in your relationship with the other party?

options.label: Yes — I experienced abuse from the other party
Yes — the other party has alleged that I was abusive
Yes — both of the above apply
No — there has been no domestic violence or abuse

fields.label: Describe the abuse you experienced
fields.placeholder: e.g. During our relationship, the other party pushed and grabbed me on multiple occasions. The most serious incident occurred in November 2023 when they shoved me against the wall in front of the children. I called police on that occasion. There were also incidents of controlling behavior — monitoring my phone, preventing me from seeing family, and controlling our finances.
fields.helpText: Describe specific incidents where possible, including approximate dates and whether the children were present or witnessed the abuse.

fields.label: Describe what the other party has alleged about you
fields.placeholder: e.g. The other party has alleged that I was physically abusive during our relationship. I deny these allegations. They made a report to police in February 2024 which was investigated and no charges were laid.

fields.label: Was police ever called for a domestic violence incident involving you and the other party?
Describe the police involvement
fields.placeholder: e.g. Police were called on November 12, 2023 following an incident at our home. The other party was charged with assault. The charge was subsequently withdrawn. Police were also called in February 2024 on a report by the other party — I was not charged.

fields.label: Is there a restraining order or peace bond between you and the other party?

options.label: Yes — there is a restraining order against the other party
Yes — there is a restraining order against me
Yes — there is a peace bond

fields.label: Describe the restraining order or peace bond
fields.placeholder: e.g. There is a restraining order made February 15, 2024 by Justice Chen prohibiting the other party from attending within 200 metres of my home and the children's school. It expires February 14, 2026.

fields.label: Were the children present for or affected by any of the incidents described above?
Describe how the children were affected
fields.placeholder: e.g. The children (ages 7 and 9) were present in the home during the November 2023 incident and witnessed the other party pushing me. Alex has since disclosed to a school counsellor that they are afraid of the other party. Both children are currently receiving counselling.

steps.title: Proposed Parenting Plan
steps.description: Describe your proposed parenting plan and why it is in the best interests of the children.

fields.label: Describe your proposed parenting arrangement in detail
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am proposing that Alex and Sam live primarily with me at my home in Toronto. I propose that the other party have parenting time every other weekend from Friday at 6 p.m. to Sunday at 6 p.m., plus one evening per week (Wednesdays from 4 p.m. to 7 p.m.). Holidays to be shared equally as follows: [describe holiday schedule]. I am asking for sole decision-making responsibility because the parties are unable to communicate effectively and agreement on major decisions is not feasible.
fields.helpText: Be specific about where the children will live, how often each parent will see them, and who will make major decisions about education, health care, and religion.

fields.label: What are you proposing for decision-making responsibility?

options.label: Sole decision-making to me — I make all major decisions
Joint decision-making — both parties decide together
Parallel decision-making — each parent decides independently in their area (e.g. I decide education, they decide health)
Sole decision-making to the other party (I am only seeking parenting time)

fields.label: Why is your proposed arrangement in the best interests of the children?
fields.placeholder: e.g. I have been the children's primary caregiver throughout their lives. I manage their school attendance, medical appointments, and extracurricular activities. The children are settled in their current school and community. My proposed arrangement maintains stability and allows both children to maintain a meaningful relationship with both parents while ensuring their safety and well-being.
fields.helpText: Ontario law requires the court to focus on the best interests of the children. Address: stability, existing relationships, safety, each parent's ability to meet the children's needs, and the children's own views (if age-appropriate).

fields.label: Have the children expressed views or preferences about where they want to live or how much time they want to spend with each parent?

options.label: No / too young to express a view

fields.label: Describe the children's views
fields.placeholder: e.g. Alex (age 12) has expressed clearly to me, to their school counsellor, and to a family friend that they want to live primarily with me and see the other party on weekends. Sam (age 9) has said they miss the other party but is settled and happy living with me.

fields.label: Describe the children's current relationship with the other parent
fields.placeholder: e.g. The children love both parents and have a positive relationship with the other party despite the separation. They enjoy their time with the other party and I support that relationship. However, the other party has cancelled parenting time on four occasions in the past two months without adequate notice, which has been upsetting for the children.

fields.label: Are you willing to support the children's relationship with the other parent?

options.label: Yes — I am committed to supporting the children's relationship with both parents
Yes — but with conditions due to safety concerns
No — I have serious concerns about the other parent's access to the children

fields.label: Describe the conditions or concerns
fields.placeholder: e.g. I support the children having a relationship with the other party but believe all visits should be supervised by a neutral third party given the history of domestic violence. I am asking the court to order supervised access until a parenting assessment has been completed.

steps.title: Declaration
steps.description: This affidavit must be sworn before a commissioner of oaths. Do not sign it until you are in front of the commissioner.

fields.label: Will you swear or affirm?
Municipality where you will swear or affirm this affidavit
fields.placeholder: e.g. Toronto

fields.label: Date of commissioning (leave blank to complete at courthouse)
Declaration
fields.text: I, the deponent, declare that the contents of this affidavit are true, and I make this solemn declaration conscientiously believing it to be true and knowing that it is of the same force and effect as if made under oath. I understand that providing false information in an affidavit is perjury.
fields.checkboxLabel: I confirm this declaration is true and complete to the best of my knowledge and belief.

pdfMapping.notes: Form 35.1 — Affidavit in Support of Claim for Custody or Access. Must be sworn before a commissioner of oaths. Required whenever custody or access is claimed in an Ontario family law proceeding. See ontario.ca/page/family-law-forms for current version.
